result.markdown:
---
title: Initial GAM exploration
date: last-modified
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
---
@secor2025 provided a simple generalized additive model (GAM) evaluated in R [@r_2026] for the analysis of marine fauna flyways. In the manuscript, the average latitude per individual, per week of a Hudson River population of striped bass _Morone saxatilis_, possibly the entire population of North Atlantic right whale _Eubalaena glacialis_, and several populations of northern gannet _Morus bassanus_ were regressed against date. We reproduce that analysis here for an expanded data set of striped bass locations which includes Potomac and Delaware populations as well as for tagged sand tiger sharks _Carcharias taurus_. We do not report on _Atlantic sturgeon_ here as the data set has not been fully-compiled as of 2026-04-30.

We will use the following packages:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(arrow) # Data import
library(dplyr) # Data manipulation
library(sf) # Spatial
library(ggplot2) # Data visualization
library(mgcv) # GAM fitting
library(gratia) # GAM visualization

theme_set(theme_minimal())
```
:::


## Striped bass _Morone saxatilis_


::: {.cell}

:::


We first visualize the distribution of the median per-individual latitudes through time:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(sb) +
  geom_point(aes(x = wk, y = med_lat, color = group), alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~year) +
  labs(x = "Week of year", y = "Weekly median latitude") +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Weekly median latitude of striped bass. Colors represent putative populations; DE: Delaware, HR: Hudson River, PR: Potomac River.](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-plot-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Following our projectiong selection procedure in the [Projection Error chapter](projection_error.qmd), we project the data into an azimuthal equidistant projection (AEQD) and rotate the data to "migration north". To project into AEQD, we first need a projection center. This will be the median receiver location.


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 2
   lon0  lat0
  <dbl> <dbl>
1 -73.9  40.7
```


:::
:::


Create the [PROJ](https://proj.org/en/stable/index.html) string...

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
proj_string <- paste0(
  "+proj=aeqd +lon_0=",
  round(projection_center$lon0, 3),
  " +lat_0=",
  round(projection_center$lat0, 3)
)

proj_string
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "+proj=aeqd +lon_0=-73.899 +lat_0=40.651"
```


:::
:::

...and project the fish locations:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sb_sf <- sb |>
  st_as_sf(coords = c("med_lon", "med_lat"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE) |>
  st_transform(proj_string)

sb_sf |>
  distinct(med_lon, med_lat, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf() +
  coord_sf(datum = proj_string)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Distribution of striped bass detections in an AEQD projection centered on the median receiver location.](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-aeqd-plot-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


We now conduct principle components analysis (PCA) on the major migration vector to find its angle from true north: the _migration azimuth_. 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
migration_azimuth <- sb_sf |>
  # Extract AEQD coordinates
  st_coordinates() |>
  # Run PCA
  prcomp() |>
  # Extract PCA loadings
  _$rotation |>
  # Transpose matrix
  t() |>
  # Find coordinates of a second location on the X axis.
  # Here we use 1 km but it could be any value
  (\(.) c(1e3, 0) %*% .)() |>
  # Find the angle between true north and migration north
  (\(.) atan2(.[1], .[2]) * 180 / pi)()

# Measure clockwise from north
migration_azimuth <- ifelse(
  migration_azimuth < 0,
  180 + migration_azimuth,
  migration_azimuth
)
```
:::


So, the migration vector is rotated 43$\degree$ from north. To rotate the data, we're going to lie to the program and say that the data are currently projected around the north pole, then add the migration azimuth to it.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
st_crs(sb_sf) <- "+proj=aeqd +lat_0=90 +lon_0=0"
proj_rotated <- paste0("+proj=aeqd +lat_0=90 +lon_0=", migration_azimuth)

sb_migration_north <- sb_sf |>
  st_transform(proj_rotated) |>
  # extract coordinates for modeling purposes
  mutate(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2])


ggplot(sb_migration_north) +
  geom_sf() +
  coord_sf(datum = proj_rotated)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-rotate-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


We can now visualize migration synchrony, the variance in the migration vector over time, and migration front, variance in excursion from the migration vector over time [@secor2025].


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(sb_migration_north) +
  geom_point(aes(x = wk, y = x, color = group), alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~year) +
  labs(
    title = "Migration synchrony",
    x = "Week of year",
    y = "Distance along the migration vector"
  ) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-synchrony-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(sb_migration_north) +
  geom_point(aes(x = wk, y = y, color = group), alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~year) +
  labs(
    title = "Migration front",
    x = "Week of year",
    y = "Distance across the migration vector"
  ) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-front-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### Initial GAM
#### Migration vector

For the initial GAM, we'll treat both year and population as a random effect. We see a general trend of low values on the migration vector through the beginning of the year, a rapid increase after the 10th week, a slight reduction in rate in week 15-20, then another rapid increase through week 25. Posiiton then levels off through week 40, where it rapidly drops.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
model_data <- sb_migration_north |>
  mutate(group = factor(group), yr_f = factor(year))

m1 <- model_data |>
  gam(
    x ~ s(wk, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re") + s(group, bs = "re"),
    knots = list(wk = c(1, 53)),
    data = _,
    method = "REML"
  )

summary(m1)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Family: gaussian 
Link function: identity 

Formula:
x ~ s(wk, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re") + s(group, 
    bs = "re")

Parametric coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
(Intercept)    13190      25041   0.527    0.598

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
            edf Ref.df       F p-value    
s(wk)    20.769     25 15255.3  <2e-16 ***
s(yr_f)   6.178      7   158.5  <2e-16 ***
s(group)  1.993      2  1481.9  <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

R-sq.(adj) =  0.877   Deviance explained = 87.7%
-REML =  95557  Scale est. = 8.5288e+09  n = 7431
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
draw(m1, select = "s(wk)", residuals = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-gam-all-re-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


Next we'll pull the populations apart and treat them as "by-variable smooths" (i.e. one form of a GAM interaction) in the model. We see that the leveling-off of movement up the migration vector in weeks 15-20 is likely due to spawning. The Potomac River has a leveling-off earlier and lower on the vector, followed by Delaware a little higher and later, then Hudson higher and greater still. **These _destination habitats_ are one of the migration metrics we wish to quantify.**


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
m2 <- model_data |>
  gam(
    x ~ group + s(wk, by = group, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re"),
    knots = list(wk = c(1, 53)),
    data = _,
    method = "REML"
  )


summary(m2)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Family: gaussian 
Link function: identity 

Formula:
x ~ group + s(wk, by = group, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re")

Parametric coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)    65245       6060   10.77   <2e-16 ***
groupPR       -84158       3061  -27.50   <2e-16 ***
groupDE       -60663       2190  -27.70   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
                 edf Ref.df       F p-value    
s(wk):groupHR 22.729     25 3029.41  <2e-16 ***
s(wk):groupPR 18.788     25  714.35  <2e-16 ***
s(wk):groupDE 19.868     25 1838.72  <2e-16 ***
s(yr_f)        5.955      7   10.55  <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

R-sq.(adj) =  0.912   Deviance explained = 91.3%
-REML =  94348  Scale est. = 6.0601e+09  n = 7431
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
draw(m2, select = c("s(wk)"), partial_match = TRUE, residuals = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-gam-re-yr-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


#### Migration front

We'll do the same with the migration front (the deviation from the migration vector).


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
m1_mf <- model_data |>
  gam(
    y ~ s(wk, k = 53, bs = "cc") +
      s(yr_f, bs = "re") +
      s(group, bs = "re"),
    knots = list(wk = c(1, 53)),
    data = _,
    method = "REML"
  )

summary(m1_mf)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Family: gaussian 
Link function: identity 

Formula:
y ~ s(wk, k = 53, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re") + s(group, 
    bs = "re")

Parametric coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)   -76170       6319  -12.05   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
            edf Ref.df       F p-value    
s(wk)    33.093     51 453.827 < 2e-16 ***
s(yr_f)   4.196      7   8.516 0.00757 ** 
s(group)  1.975      2 172.239 < 2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

R-sq.(adj) =  0.722   Deviance explained = 72.3%
-REML =  90614  Scale est. = 2.2479e+09  n = 7431
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
draw(m1_mf, select = "s(wk)", residuals = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-gam-front-all-re-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
m2_mf <- model_data |>
  gam(
    y ~ group +
      s(wk, by = group, k = 53, bs = "cc") +
      s(yr_f, bs = "re"),
    knots = list(wk = c(1, 53)),
    data = _,
    method = "REML"
  )

summary(m2_mf)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Family: gaussian 
Link function: identity 

Formula:
y ~ group + s(wk, by = group, k = 53, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re")

Parametric coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)   -61409       2554  -24.04   <2e-16 ***
groupPR       -24869       1626  -15.29   <2e-16 ***
groupDE       -18397       1176  -15.64   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
                 edf Ref.df      F p-value    
s(wk):groupHR 34.048     51 535.42  <2e-16 ***
s(wk):groupPR 19.893     51  49.60  <2e-16 ***
s(wk):groupDE 22.237     51 100.04  <2e-16 ***
s(yr_f)        5.518      7  13.63  <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

R-sq.(adj) =  0.783   Deviance explained = 78.6%
-REML =  89740  Scale est. = 1.75e+09  n = 7431
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
draw(m2_mf, select = "s(wk)", partial_match = TRUE, residuals = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-gam-front-re-yr-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### Model evaluation
It should be noted, however, that the initial models are not performing well on the edges of the domain of the migration vector and migration front. This suggests that we need to refine the model to further to characterize these areas.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
appraise(m1)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-appraise-1.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
appraise(m2)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-appraise-2.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
appraise(m1_mf)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-appraise-3.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
appraise(m2_mf)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/sb-appraise-4.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Sand tiger shark _Carcharias taurus_


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sandtiger <- open_dataset("../data/RAW/sandtigers.csv", format = "csv") |>
  mutate(
    datetime = strptime(DateandTime, format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M", tz = "UTC"),
    wk = week(datetime),
    yr = year(datetime)
  ) |>
  group_by(Transmitter, wk, yr) |>
  summarize(
    med_lon = median(Longitude, na.rm = TRUE),
    med_lat = median(Latitude, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  collect()
```
:::




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(sandtiger) +
  geom_point(aes(x = wk, y = med_lat), alpha = 0.4, color = "orange") +
  facet_wrap(~yr) +
  labs(x = "Week of year", y = "Weekly median latitude") +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Weekly median latitude of sand tiger sharks.](initial_gam_files/figure-html/st-plot-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 1 × 2
   lon0  lat0
  <dbl> <dbl>
1 -75.1  38.7
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
proj_string <- paste0(
  "+proj=aeqd +lon_0=",
  round(projection_center$lon0, 3),
  " +lat_0=",
  round(projection_center$lat0, 3)
)

proj_string
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "+proj=aeqd +lon_0=-75.117 +lat_0=38.743"
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
sandtiger_sf <- sandtiger |>
  st_as_sf(coords = c("med_lon", "med_lat"), crs = 4326) |>
  st_transform(proj_string)

sandtiger_sf |>
  distinct(geometry) |>
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf() +
  coord_sf(datum = proj_string)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![Distribution of sand tiger detections in an AEQD projection centered on the median receiver location.](initial_gam_files/figure-html/st-aeqd-plot-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
migration_azimuth <- sandtiger_sf |>
  # Extract AEQD coordinates
  st_coordinates() |>
  # Run PCA
  prcomp() |>
  # Extract PCA loadings
  _$rotation |>
  # Transpose matrix
  t() |>
  # Find coordinates of a second location on the X axis.
  # Here we use 1 km but it could be any value
  (\(.) c(1e3, 0) %*% .)() |>
  # Find the angle between true north and migration north
  (\(.) atan2(.[1], .[2]) * 180 / pi)()

# Measure clockwise from north
migration_azimuth <- ifelse(
  migration_azimuth < 0,
  180 + migration_azimuth,
  migration_azimuth
)

migration_azimuth
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 25.09106
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
st_crs(sandtiger_sf) <- "+proj=aeqd +lat_0=90 +lon_0=0"
proj_rotated <- paste0("+proj=aeqd +lat_0=90 +lon_0=", migration_azimuth)

sandtiger_migration_north <- sandtiger_sf |>
  st_transform(proj_rotated) |>
  # extract coordinates for modeling purposes
  mutate(x = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1], y = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2])


ggplot(sandtiger_migration_north) +
  geom_sf() +
  coord_sf(datum = proj_rotated)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/st-rotate-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(sandtiger_migration_north) +
  geom_point(aes(x = wk, y = x), alpha = 0.4, color = "orange") +
  facet_wrap(~yr) +
  labs(
    title = "Migration synchrony",
    x = "Week of year",
    y = "Distance along the migration vector"
  ) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/st-synchrony-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(sandtiger_migration_north) +
  geom_point(aes(x = wk, y = y, color = group), alpha = 0.4, color = "orange") +
  facet_wrap(~yr) +
  labs(
    title = "Migration front",
    x = "Week of year",
    y = "Distance across the migration vector"
  ) +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 12))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/st-front-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


### Initial GAM
#### Migration vector



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
model_data <- sandtiger_migration_north |>
  mutate(yr_f = factor(yr))

m1_st <- model_data |>
  gam(
    x ~ s(wk, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re"),
    knots = list(wk = c(1, 53)),
    data = _,
    method = "REML"
  )

summary(m1_st)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Link function: identity 

Formula:
x ~ s(wk, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re")

Parametric coefficients:

(Intercept)   -55237      24765   -2.23   0.0258 *
---

Approximate significance of smooth terms:
          edf Ref.df       F p-value    
s(wk)   24.01     25 4328.16  <2e-16 ***
s(yr_f) 10.64     11   43.91  <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

R-sq.(adj) =  0.574   Deviance explained = 57.7%
-REML =  71046  Scale est. = 2.9382e+09  n = 5759
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
draw(m1_st, select = "s(wk)", residuals = TRUE)
```

```
R-sq.(adj) =  0.874   Deviance explained = 87.7%
-REML =  67705  Scale est. = 8.7016e+08  n = 5759
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
draw(m2_st, select = c("s(wk)"), partial_match = TRUE, residuals = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](initial_gam_files/figure-html/st-gam-by-yr-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


#### Migration front

We'll do the same with the migration front (the deviation from the migration vector).

::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
m1_mf <- model_data |>
  gam(
    y ~ s(wk, k = 27, bs = "cc") + s(yr_f, bs = "re"),
    knots = list(wk = c(1, 53)),
    data = _,
    method = "REML"
  )

summary(m1_mf)
```

```{.r .cell-code}
summary(m2_mf)
```
